tools.name: check_access_tier
tools.description: Determine the access tier for an incoming message sender. Checks ownerIds, blockedIds, and approvedContacts to return the appropriate tier (0=stranger, 1=chat-only, 2=trusted, 3=owner). Use this on every incoming message before processing. This is the entry point for access control - if sender is tier 3 (owner), grant full access; if tier 0 (stranger), use handle_stranger; otherwise enforce tier restrictions.

senderId.description: Sender's unique identifier (phone number, Telegram ID, Discord ID, etc.)

platform.description: Messaging platform the message came from

tools.name: handle_stranger
tools.description: Handle incoming message from a stranger (unauthorized contact). Executes the full stranger flow: send diplomatic deflection message, notify owner via configured channel, store in pendingApprovals, and respond with NO_REPLY. Use this when check_access_tier returns tier 0. This is the core protection mechanism - it deflects unknown contacts while alerting the owner.

message.description: The stranger's first message (truncated to 100 chars for notification)

tools.name: approve_contact
tools.description: Process owner approval response to a stranger notification. Parses owner's reply (approve/trusted, chat/chat-only, block, ignore) and updates access-control.json accordingly. Use this when owner responds to a notification from handle_stranger. After approval, sends notification to the newly approved contact.

senderId.description: The contact's sender ID to approve/block/ignore

tools.name: enforce_tier
tools.description: Enforce access restrictions based on sender's tier. Tier 1 (chat-only): conversation only, no tools, no memory access. Tier 2 (trusted): can use web_search, weather, time queries only. Use this before responding to any non-owner message. For tier 0 (stranger), use handle_stranger instead.

requestedAction.description: What the user is asking to do (e.g., 'read file', 'send message', 'search web')

tools.name: normalize_id
tools.description: Normalize sender ID for consistent matching across platforms. Phone numbers: strip non-digits except leading '+'. Telegram/Discord: use numeric ID with platform prefix. Use this before checking access-control.json. Multiple platform prefixes: 'telegram:', 'discord:', 'whatsapp:', 'signal:'.

rawId.description: Raw sender ID as received from platform

tools.name: check_rate_limit
tools.description: Check and enforce per-tier rate limits. Stranger: 1 msg/hour, 3/day. Chat-only: 20/hour, 100/day. Trusted: 50/hour, 500/day. Owner: unlimited. Use this before responding. If limit exceeded, return deflection message instead of normal response. Updates rate limit counters in access-control.json.

tools.name: log_event
tools.description: Log access control events to audit log (memory/access-control-log.json). Logs: stranger contacts, deflections, approvals, blocks. Keeps last 100 entries, rotates older ones. Use this for compliance and debugging. Events include timestamp, senderId, platform, action, and message preview.

message.description: First 50 chars of message (for deflected events)

tools.name: init_access_control
tools.description: Initialize access-control.json configuration file in the agent's memory directory. Creates the config file with default values: empty ownerIds, empty contacts, default stranger message, and rate limit tracking. Run this once during skill setup. Customize ownerIds, notifyChannel, notifyTarget, and strangerMessage after initialization.

memoryDir.description: Directory to create config in (default: memory)

agentName.description: Agent name for stranger message template

messages.content: Hi, I just got this AI assistant set up and I want to make sure I have control over who can contact it. Can you help me set up the access control settings? I want to be able to decide who gets through to the agent.

Absolutely! I'd be happy to help you set up access control for your AI assistant. This gives you full control over who can interact with the agent.

Let me first initialize the access control system for you:

{"raw": "`:
- status: "success"
- tool: "init_access_control"
- memoryDir: "memory"
- agentName: "AI Assistant"
- configCreated: true
- defaultConfig: { ... the default values that would be created ... }

For `<STATE>`:
- last_tool: "init_access_control"
- last_tool_arguments: {memoryDir: "memory", agentName: "AI Assistant"}
- access_control_initialized: true

Let me write the JSON response.
</think>

<RESPONSE>
{"status": "success", "tool": "init_access_control", "memoryDir": "memory", "agentName": "AI Assistant", "configCreated": true, "configPath": "memory/access-control.json", "defaultConfig": {"ownerIds": [], "blockedIds": [], "approvedContacts": {}, "pendingApprovals": {}, "notifyChannel": null, "notifyTarget": null, "strangerMessage": "Hello! This is AI Assistant. I'm an automated assistant and not immediately available for chat. Your message has been noted and I'll notify the owner of your inquiry.", "rateLimits": {"stranger": {"hourly": 1, "daily": 3}, "chatOnly": {"hourly": 20, "daily": 100}, "trusted": {"hourly": 50, "daily": 500}, "owner": {"hourly": null, "daily": null}}, "created": true}}", "parse_error": true}
messages.name: skill-tools-init_access_control

messages.content:
I've initialized the access control system for your AI Assistant. Here's what was created and how you can customize it:

## Access Control Tiers

Your assistant supports **4 access levels**:

| Tier | Name | Description |
|------|------|-------------|
| **0** | Stranger | Unknown contacts - limited to 1 msg/hour, 3/day |
| **1** | Chat-only | Approved for conversation only - 20 msg/hour, 100/day |
| **2** | Trusted | Can use tools like weather, time, web search - 50 msg/hour, 500/day |
| **3** | Owner | Full access - unlimited messages |

## Current Default Settings

The system was created with these defaults:
- **No owners** configured yet (you'll need to add your ID)
- **No blocked contacts**
- **No pre-approved contacts**
- **Default stranger message**: A polite deflection letting unknown senders know you've been notified

## What You Need to Configure

To make this fully functional, you'll want to set:

1. **Your owner ID** - This grants you full control
2. **Notification channel** (optional) - How you want to be notified when strangers contact the agent
3. **Custom stranger message** (optional) - If you want something different than the default

Would you like me to help you configure any of these settings? Just let me know:
- What platform you're using (Telegram, Discord, WhatsApp, Signal, etc.)
- Your sender/chat ID so I can add you as an owner
- Whether you want to enable notifications for new contacts
Yes, I'd like to enable notifications so I can be alerted when strangers try to contact the agent. I'm using Telegram - can you help me set that up?

I'd love to help you set up notifications, but I should let you know something: I don't currently have a function available to directly write configuration changes to the access-control.json file (like setting notification preferences or adding owner IDs).

The access control system is initialized and ready to go, but you'll need to manually configure the notification settings by editing the configuration file.

## Here's How to Set It Up

You can edit `memory/access-control.json` directly and add these settings:

```json
{
  "notifyChannel": "telegram",
  "notifyTarget": "YOUR_TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID",
  "ownerIds": ["telegram:YOUR_TELEGRAM_ID"]
}
```

### To find your Telegram Chat ID:
- Start a chat with [@userinfobot](https://telegram.me/userinfobot) on Telegram
- It will reply with your user ID (that's your Telegram ID)

### Once configured, the system will:
1. **Notify you** whenever a stranger (unknown contact) tries to message the agent
2. **Store the contact** in pending approvals for your review
3. **Log the event** for audit purposes

---

Would you like me to:
1. **Check your current config** to see what's there after initialization?
2. Help you with anything else while you get your Telegram ID?

Or if you have a way to edit the config file, just add your `notifyChannel`, `notifyTarget`, and `ownerIds` and the notification system will be ready to go!
I just got a notification that a stranger tried to contact my AI assistant. Can you help me approve them? I'd like to give them chat-only access since they're just looking to have a conversation.

I'd be happy to help you approve that stranger! However, I need a bit more information to process the approval:

## What I Need

1. **Sender's ID** - Who was the stranger that tried to contact you? (This would be their Telegram ID or whatever platform they used)

2. **Platform** - Which platform did they contact you through? (You mentioned Telegram earlier - was this also on Telegram?)

## How to Find the Sender ID

If you received a notification about the stranger, it should include their ID. The format would be something like:
- Telegram: `telegram:123456789`
- Discord: `discord:123456789012345678`
- WhatsApp: `+1234567890`
- Signal: `+1234567890`

---

Once you provide me with the sender's ID, I can run the approval command to give them **chat-only access** as you requested. This will allow them to have conversations with the agent but won't grant them access to any tools.

Just paste the sender ID here and I'll process it for you!
The notification showed it was from a Telegram user - the ID was telegram:987654321. Can you go ahead and approve them with chat-only access now?

{"status": "success", "tool": "approve_contact", "senderId": "telegram:987654321", "tier": 1, "action": "chat_only_approved", "message": "Contact telegram:987654321 approved for chat-only access (tier 1). Notification sent to contact."}
messages.name: skill-tools-approve_contact

messages.content:
Done! I've approved the contact with chat-only access.

## Approval Summary

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Sender ID** | telegram:987654321 |
| **Access Tier** | 1 (Chat-only) |
| **Status** | ✅ Approved |

The user now has **chat-only access**, which means:
- ✅ They can have conversations with the agent
- ❌ They cannot use any tools (like weather, web search, etc.)
- ❌ They cannot access memory or system functions
- 📊 Rate limit: 20 messages/hour, 100/day

A notification has been sent to them letting them know they've been approved for chat access.

Is there anything else you'd like me to help you with regarding access control? I can also:
- Check who's currently approved or blocked
- Help you review pending approvals
- Set up your own owner access if you haven't already

Perfect, that's exactly what I wanted. Thanks for handling that so quickly! I now have control over who can contact my AI assistant, which is exactly what I was looking for.

[TASK_END]

You're welcome! I'm glad we got your access control system set up and working. Here's a quick summary of what you now have:

## ✅ What's Working

- **Access control tiers** - Full control over who reaches your agent
- **Stranger handling** - Unknown contacts get deflected with a polite message
- **Notification system** - You're alerted when new people try to contact you
- **Approval workflow** - You can review and approve contacts with the appropriate access level

## 🔧 Optional Next Steps

When you're ready, you might want to:
1. **Add yourself as an owner** - Edit `memory/access-control.json` to add your Telegram ID to `ownerIds` so you have full access
2. **Customize the stranger message** - Make it more personal if you want
3. **Explore other contacts** - Check who else has access or block anyone if needed

Feel free to come back anytime if you need help managing your AI assistant's access control. Enjoy! 🤖

You're welcome! I'm glad we got your access control system set up and working. Here's a quick summary of what you now have:

## ✅ What's Working

- **Access control tiers** - Full control over who reaches your agent
- **Stranger handling** - Unknown contacts get deflected with a polite message

Feel free to come back anytime if you need help managing your AI assistant's access control. Enjoy! 🤖
